feed.headline: EU agrees landmark AI governance roadmap, but implementation timeline remains contested
feed.ai: Commission and member states outline common AI liability and certification rules while national regulators still negotiate enforcement powers.
feed.frag: Highlights ongoing fragmentation in digital regulation
feed.fed: Moves the federation closer to a single EU tech governance framework

feed.headline: European Parliament backs strengthened carbon border levy for imported steel and chemicals
feed.ai: A new CBAM proposal seeks tighter enforcement on high-carbon imports, raising pressure on industry and trading partners.
feed.frag: Increases trade tensions under a fractured climate policy regime
feed.fed: Supports the federal green-industrial transition by aligning external carbon pricing

feed.headline: EU foreign ministers approve a joint connectivity package for the Western Balkans
feed.ai: Funding and regulatory alignment for cross-border energy and data corridors are agreed, signalling deeper integration with candidate neighbours.
feed.frag: Limited by separate national implementation plans
feed.fed: Accelerates the federation’s eastern enlargement and infrastructure integration

feed.headline: Council debates migration and energy solidarity rules ahead of summer peak
feed.ai: Member states are split over mandatory burden-sharing and fast-track renewable power sharing, exposing persistent EU coordination gaps.
feed.frag: Highlights the limits of national-only crisis response
feed.fed: Reinforces the need for a federal emergency energy and asylum framework

feed.headline: Council fails to agree on unified chip-export control list
feed.ai: Member states retain national veto over semiconductor export rules, delaying a common EU position on technology controls.

feed.headline: European Commission publishes new Capital Markets Union roadmap
feed.ai: A fresh roadmap proposes common rules for cross-border securities settlement, aiming to unlock pooled investment for green and tech industry by 2030.
feed.frag: Limited near-term effect if national implementation stalls
feed.fed: Modest positive — concrete step toward a unified capital market

feed.headline: Western Balkans summit reaffirms 2030 accession ambition
feed.ai: Leaders restate a target of opening final accession chapters with Montenegro and Albania, while flagging rule-of-law conditions still unmet by several candidates.
feed.fed: Positive — keeps the Balkans accession track alive

feed.headline: EU and US extend tech standards dialogue, no binding agreement
feed.ai: Talks on AI and data governance continue without a binding transatlantic framework, leaving the EU reliant on US cloud and AI infrastructure for now.
feed.frag: Confirms continued dependency on foreign technology
feed.fed: No immediate change — sovereignty push continues domestically

feed.headline: Ukraine accession talks: energy chapter provisionally closed
feed.ai: Negotiators provisionally close the energy chapter of Ukraine's accession talks, citing progress on grid synchronisation with the EU network.
feed.frag: Accession progress remains partial and dependent on external funding

feed.headline: Hungary blocks joint statement on foreign policy coordination
feed.ai: A member state veto again prevents a unified EU position, illustrating the limits of unanimity-based foreign policy decision-making.
